title: Avoid CUDA dependency download by using --system-site-packages with pre-installed torch-npu
problem_description: Installing requirements.txt (torch, torch-npu, torchvision) from PyPI pulled PyTorch 2.11.0 with the full NVIDIA CUDA toolkit stack (cuda-toolkit, nvidia-cudnn, nvidia-nccl, etc.), totaling over 1GB. This exceeded the 300s timeout even with 阿里云 mirror, because CUDA packages are unnecessary on an Ascend NPU host.
rough_fix_approach: 1. Before pip install, check if torch-npu is already available system-wide. 2. If yes, create venv with --system-site-packages flag instead of clean venv. 3. Verify torch.npu.is_available() returns True inside the venv. 4. Skip pip install of torch-related packages entirely — inherit system versions.
reasoning: Highly generalizable: any CUDA-to-NPU migration running on Ascend infrastructure with pre-installed torch-npu should use --system-site-packages to avoid downloading the entire NVIDIA CUDA stack. This is a recurring environment setup pattern that will save 5+ minutes per migration run.
